The comfort of parents depends greatly on their children; and this encourages both to fulfill their duties.
Although a righteous person may be poor, the Lord will not allow them to lack what is necessary for spiritual life.
Those who are enthusiastic in spirit, serving the Lord, are likely to have rich faith and perform many good works.
Here is just blame for those who waste opportunities, both now and in the future.
A great number of blessings will remain with good people; real blessings.
Both the righteous and the wicked must die; but there is a vast difference between their souls.
A wise person puts their knowledge into practice.
Deceivers, after all their schemes, will be exposed.
Trickery and deceit will not excuse wrongdoing.
A good person’s mouth is always open to teach, comfort, and correct others.
Where there is hatred, everything stirs up conflict. By bearing with each other, peace and harmony are preserved.
Those who foolishly continue in wicked ways prepare punishment for themselves.

Whatever knowledge may be useful, we must store it away so that it will not be difficult to find when we need it. Wise people gain this wisdom by reading, hearing the word, meditating, praying, and having faith in Christ, who is made from God our wisdom.

This refers to common mistakes of both rich and poor, regarding their external circumstances. Wealth exposes wealthy people to many dangers; while a poor person may live comfortably if they are content, have a good conscience, and live by faith.
Perhaps a righteous person has no more than what they work hard for, but that labor leads to life.
A traveler who has lost their way and cannot bear to be told of it or shown the right path will continue to stray.
It is especially foolish to think you can hide anything from God; and malice is no better.
Those who speak a lot often speak incorrectly. A person who controls themselves is wise, and does that for their own peace.

The words of a righteous person are sincere, free from deceit and evil intentions. Pious conversation is spiritual nourishment to those in need. Fools die because they lack understanding, as the word says; because they lack thought.
True wealth has no anxiety when enjoyed, no grief over loss, and no guilt through misuse. What comes from love of God has God’s grace accompanying it.
Only foolish and wicked people enjoy harming others or tempting them to sin.
The greatest blessings that the righteous can desire will be granted to them.
The way of prosperous sinners is like a whirlwind, which quickly spends itself and disappears.
Just as vinegar irritates the teeth, and smoke stings the eyes, so a lazy person annoys their employer.
What person loves life? Let them fear God, and that will secure enough life for them in this world, and eternal life in the next.
A believer grows stronger in faith and obeys with increased joy.
The wicked would be glad to have this earth as their home forever, but it cannot be so. They must die and leave all their idols behind.

A good person speaks wisely for the benefit of others. But it is sin, and will be the ruin of a wicked person, that they speak what displeases God and provokes those they converse with. The righteous is kept by the power of God; and nothing will be able to separate them from the love of God in Christ Jesus.
